normalized_title: City of Melville Local Planning Policy 1.21 - Short-Term Rental Accommodation
scope_summary: Sets out the City of Melville's locational and operational assessment criteria for short-term rental accommodation (STRA) land uses that still require development approval after the State's 2024 STRA planning reforms (hosted STRA and registered unhosted STRA used no more than 90 nights per 12 months in the metropolitan region are exempt). In Residential zones STRA is generally not supported unless preferred property and operational characteristics are met and no undesirable characteristics apply; mixed-use zones and non-residential parts of activity centres are preferred. Also covers car parking, signage, operational management plans, consultation (including strata consent) and temporary approvals under clause 72 of Schedule 2 of the Regulations.

key_numeric_standards.applies_to: registered unhosted short-term rental accommodation in the metropolitan region (state exemption restated in policy scope)

key_numeric_standards.topic: preferred proximity to mixed use zones / activity centres
key_numeric_standards.applies_to: STRA in the Residential zone (preferred property characteristic)

key_numeric_standards.topic: preferred proximity to hospital or tertiary education institution
key_numeric_standards.applies_to: STRA in the Residential zone (preferred property characteristic)
STRA in the Residential zone (preferred operational characteristic)
STRA in the Residential zone (preferred operational characteristic)
discrete sign near dwelling entry in Residential zones (no other on-site signage permitted)

quality_flags: clean extraction; manifest instrument_name says 'Short Term Accommodation' while the policy's formal citation is 'Local Planning Policy No. 1.21 - Short Term Rental Accommodation'
definitions section lists terms defined elsewhere (Regulations, LPS6, R-Codes) by reference rather than reproducing the definitions
